It holds the status of a former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] and is one of the sites connected to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} tradition. == Overview == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, was relocated from {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}} to the {{ill|Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, according to legend. Toyuke Daijinja is considered one of the locations associated with the Moto Ise tradition, the original site of the Gekū, and is also known as the "Moto Ise Gekū." The shrine is part of the "Moto Ise Sansha" ({{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|lt=Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|lt=Moto Ise Three Shrines|ja|元伊勢三社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto Ise Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the Ōe area of Fukuchiyama. The three shrines include: {|class="wikitable" |- ! colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Fukuchiyama, Ōe-chō, Naikū |Origin site of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Fukuchiyama, Ōe-chō, Amatanai |Origin site of the {{ill|Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Fukuchiyama, Ōe-chō, Busshōji |Inner Shrine’s Okumiya |} == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * '''{{ill|Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}''' - The deity enshrined at the {{ill|Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) ; Other Deities * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Hikohananinigi-no-Mikoto|ja|日子番能邇邇芸尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohananinigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Futodama|lt=Futodama|lt=Futodama-no-Mikoto|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}} == History == According to the {{ill|Tōyuke-sha Gishiki-chō|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyuke-sha Gishiki-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}} (804 CE), Emperor Yūryaku relocated the deity Toyouke from {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}} to {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} upon receiving a divine command from {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} in a dream. This became the Gekū of Ise Grand Shrine. The shrine claims to be the former site of this event. Other accounts suggest the shrine was established en route from [[Hinumanai Shrine|Hino-minai Shrine]] in {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}} during the deity’s journey to Ise ({{ill|Kasa-gun Gazetteer|ja|加佐郡誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa-gun Gazetteer|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Another version links the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maro|ja|麻呂子親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 当麻皇子|1=Prince Maroko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487858}}, who established it after vanquishing demons in the area ({{ill|Tango Province Historical Records|ja|丹後州宮津府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Province Historical Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine received significant support from the {{ill|Miyazu Domain|en|Miyazu Domain|WD=Q3034498|lt=Miyazu Domain|1=Miyazu Domain}}, with the lord {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakuni|ja|京極高国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakuni|1=Kyōgoku Takakuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374458}} funding the construction of the shrine's buildings in 1656 CE. In 1930, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine, and after World War II, it became affiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Grounds == File:Toyuke-daijinja haiden.JPG|'''Haiden and Honden''' (rear)
The honden features [[Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with a [[:en:Thatched roof|Thatched roof]], external {{ill|Chigi|en|Chigi|WD=Q362820|lt=Chigi|1=Chigi}}, and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}} on the ridge. Traditionally, it underwent {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengu|ja|式年遷宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}} every 60 years.Shirakawa Co., Showa 60. The current honden was reconstructed in 1874. File:Toyuke-daijinja torii.JPG|'''Torii''' - A kurokigi (blackwood) torii, similar to {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|en|Nonomiya Shrine|WD=Q501266|lt=Nonomiya Shrine|1=Nonomiya Shrine}}. * '''{{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura-den}}''' - Features {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=Kirizuma-zukuri|1=Hip roof}} with shingled roofing. == Subsidiary Shrines == === Sessha (Branch Shrines) === Located around the main hall: * '''Taga Shrine''' - Left of Haiden * '''Tsuchi Shrine''' - Right of Haiden * '''Tsukuyomi-no-Miya''' - Rear left of Honden * '''Kaze-no-Miya''' - Rear right of Honden === Massha (End Shrines) === * Total: 37 shrines :: Arranged in a U-shape around the main hall, each massha was historically maintained by one of the shrine's 37 associated families, which also served as shrine caretakers ({{ill|Onshi|lt=Onshi|lt=Oshi|ja|御師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshi|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). [[File:Motoise-gegu02.png|thumb|Diagram of shrine layout.]] == location == ; Address * Ōe-chō, Amatanai, Higashidaira 178-2, [[:en:Fukuchiyama|Fukuchiyama]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} ; Access {{underline|By Train}} * Nearest station: {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|en|Kyoto Tango Railway|WD=Q19727758|lt=Kyoto Tango Railway|1=Kyoto Tango Railway}} {{ill|Miyafuku Line|en|Miyafuku Line|WD=Q8192675|lt=Miyafuku Line|1=Miyafuku Line}} {{ill|Ōe-Kōkōmae Station|en|Ōe-Kōkōmae Station|WD=Q8081446|lt=Ōe High School-mae Station|1=Ōe-Kōkōmae Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) {{underline|By Car}} * [[Kyoto Prefectural Route 9|Kyoto Route 9 Ayabe-Ōe-Miyazu Line]] ; Nearby * '''Miyagawa''' - A river near the shrine, sharing its name with {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie Prefecture)|1=Miyagawa}} near Ise Grand Shrine. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{Cite book|author=Association of Shinto Shrines Research Division|title=Jinja Meikan|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=1963}} * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japan’s Gods – Shrines and Sacred Sites|volume=Vol. 7|series=San’in (Revised and Republished)|publisher=Hakusuisha|date=1985|isbn=978-4-560-02217-3}} * {{Cite book|author=Naokazu Miyaji, Ariyoshi Saeki (Supervisors)|title=Shinto Daijiten (Reprint Edition)|publisher=Rinsen Books|date=1969|isbn=978-4-653-01347-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hinumanai Shrine|ja|比沼麻奈為神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinumanai Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]] [[Category:Toyuke-daijinja}}* [https://www.city.fukuchiyama.lg.jp/soshiki/65/1115.html Moto Ise Gekū Toyuke Daijinja - Fukuchiyama City Official Homepage] * [https://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=6222&r=1632663304.3013 Moto Ise Gekū Toyuke Daijinja - Kyoto Prefectural Tourism Guide] * [http://motoise-naiku.com/motoise3sya.html Moto Ise Naikū Kōtaijinja Official Site - Moto Ise Three Shrines] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto Ise Three Shrines - Fukuchiyama - Sea of Kyoto (Northern Kyoto Tourism Office)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|豊受大神社|version=85751958|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:豊受大神社]] {{Commons category|Toyuke-daijinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11633342}} [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Moto-Geku Shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyo'uke Daijingu Shrine]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Toyuke-daijinja]][[ja:豊受大神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Toyuke-daijinja]][[ja:豊受大神社]] [[ja:豊受大神社]] wiki {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Toyuke Daijinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Toyukedaijinja shrine.jpg |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}[[:en:Fukuchiyama|Fukuchiyama]]-shi, Ōe-chō, Amatanai, Higashidaira 178-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.402556|N|135.151389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724|1=Toyouke-hime}} |Rank = Former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] |consecration_year = (Trad.) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, 22nd year |Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style, [[:en:Thatched roof|Thatched roof]] |Other names = Moto Ise Gekū |Festivals = [[April 26]] |Rituals = Hassaku Festival ([[September 1]]) |prefecture = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|WD=Q3355306|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Toyuke Daijinja''' ({{ill|豊受大神社|ja|豊受大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊受大神社|1=Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633342}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Ōe|Ōe]], [[:en:Fukuchiyama|Fukuchiyama]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It holds the status of a former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] and is one of the sites connected to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} tradition. == Overview == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, was relocated from {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}} to the {{ill|Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, according to legend. Toyuke Daijinja is considered one of the locations associated with the Moto Ise tradition, the original site of the Gekū, and is also known as the "Moto Ise Gekū." The shrine is part of the "Moto Ise Sansha" ({{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|lt=Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|lt=Moto Ise Three Shrines|ja|元伊勢三社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto Ise Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the Ōe area of Fukuchiyama. The three shrines include: {|class="wikitable" |- ! colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Fukuchiyama, Ōe-chō, Naikū |Origin site of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Fukuchiyama, Ōe-chō, Amatanai |Origin site of the {{ill|Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Fukuchiyama, Ōe-chō, Busshōji |Inner Shrine’s Okumiya |} == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * '''{{ill|Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}''' - The deity enshrined at the {{ill|Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) ; Other Deities * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Hikohananinigi-no-Mikoto|ja|日子番能邇邇芸尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohananinigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Futodama|lt=Futodama|lt=Futodama-no-Mikoto|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}} == History == According to the {{ill|Tōyuke-sha Gishiki-chō|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyuke-sha Gishiki-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}} (804 CE), Emperor Yūryaku relocated the deity Toyouke from {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}} to {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} upon receiving a divine command from {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} in a dream. This became the Gekū of Ise Grand Shrine. The shrine claims to be the former site of this event. Other accounts suggest the shrine was established en route from [[Hinumanai Shrine|Hino-minai Shrine]] in {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}} during the deity’s journey to Ise ({{ill|Kasa-gun Gazetteer|ja|加佐郡誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa-gun Gazetteer|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Another version links the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maro|ja|麻呂子親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 当麻皇子|1=Prince Maroko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487858}}, who established it after vanquishing demons in the area ({{ill|Tango Province Historical Records|ja|丹後州宮津府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Province Historical Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine received significant support from the {{ill|Miyazu Domain|en|Miyazu Domain|WD=Q3034498|lt=Miyazu Domain|1=Miyazu Domain}}, with the lord {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakuni|ja|京極高国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakuni|1=Kyōgoku Takakuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374458}} funding the construction of the shrine's buildings in 1656 CE. In 1930, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine, and after World War II, it became affiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Grounds == File:Toyuke-daijinja haiden.JPG|'''Haiden and Honden''' (rear)
The honden features [[Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with a [[:en:Thatched roof|Thatched roof]], external {{ill|Chigi|en|Chigi|WD=Q362820|lt=Chigi|1=Chigi}}, and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}} on the ridge. Traditionally, it underwent {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengu|ja|式年遷宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}} every 60 years.Shirakawa Co., Showa 60. The current honden was reconstructed in 1874. File:Toyuke-daijinja torii.JPG|'''Torii''' - A kurokigi (blackwood) torii, similar to {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|en|Nonomiya Shrine|WD=Q501266|lt=Nonomiya Shrine|1=Nonomiya Shrine}}. * '''{{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura-den}}''' - Features {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=Kirizuma-zukuri|1=Hip roof}} with shingled roofing. == Subsidiary Shrines == === Sessha (Branch Shrines) === Located around the main hall: * '''Taga Shrine''' - Left of Haiden * '''Tsuchi Shrine''' - Right of Haiden * '''Tsukuyomi-no-Miya''' - Rear left of Honden * '''Kaze-no-Miya''' - Rear right of Honden === Massha (End Shrines) === * Total: 37 shrines :: Arranged in a U-shape around the main hall, each massha was historically maintained by one of the shrine's 37 associated families, which also served as shrine caretakers ({{ill|Onshi|lt=Onshi|lt=Oshi|ja|御師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshi|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). [[File:Motoise-gegu02.png|thumb|Diagram of shrine layout.]] == location == ; Address * Ōe-chō, Amatanai, Higashidaira 178-2, [[:en:Fukuchiyama|Fukuchiyama]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} ; Access {{underline|By Train}} * Nearest station: {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|en|Kyoto Tango Railway|WD=Q19727758|lt=Kyoto Tango Railway|1=Kyoto Tango Railway}} {{ill|Miyafuku Line|en|Miyafuku Line|WD=Q8192675|lt=Miyafuku Line|1=Miyafuku Line}} {{ill|Ōe-Kōkōmae Station|en|Ōe-Kōkōmae Station|WD=Q8081446|lt=Ōe High School-mae Station|1=Ōe-Kōkōmae Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) {{underline|By Car}} * [[Kyoto Prefectural Route 9|Kyoto Route 9 Ayabe-Ōe-Miyazu Line]] ; Nearby * '''Miyagawa''' - A river near the shrine, sharing its name with {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie Prefecture)|1=Miyagawa}} near Ise Grand Shrine. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{Cite book|author=Association of Shinto Shrines Research Division|title=Jinja Meikan|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=1963}} * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japan’s Gods – Shrines and Sacred Sites|volume=Vol. 7|series=San’in (Revised and Republished)|publisher=Hakusuisha|date=1985|isbn=978-4-560-02217-3}} * {{Cite book|author=Naokazu Miyaji, Ariyoshi Saeki (Supervisors)|title=Shinto Daijiten (Reprint Edition)|publisher=Rinsen Books|date=1969|isbn=978-4-653-01347-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hinumanai Shrine|ja|比沼麻奈為神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinumanai Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]] [[Category:Toyuke-daijinja}}* [https://www.city.fukuchiyama.lg.jp/soshiki/65/1115.html Moto Ise Gekū Toyuke Daijinja - Fukuchiyama City Official Homepage] * [https://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=6222&r=1632663304.3013 Moto Ise Gekū Toyuke Daijinja - Kyoto Prefectural Tourism Guide] * [http://motoise-naiku.com/motoise3sya.html Moto Ise Naikū Kōtaijinja Official Site - Moto Ise Three Shrines] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto Ise Three Shrines - Fukuchiyama - Sea of Kyoto (Northern Kyoto Tourism Office)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|豊受大神社|version=85751958|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:豊受大神社]] {{Commons category|Toyuke-daijinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11633342}} [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Moto-Geku Shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyo'uke Daijingu Shrine]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
wiki {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Toyuke Daijinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Toyukedaijinja shrine.jpg |location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}[[:en:Fukuchiyama|Fukuchiyama]]-shi, Ōe-chō, Amatanai, Higashidaira 178-2 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.402556|N|135.151389|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724|1=Toyouke-hime}} |Rank = Former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] |consecration_year = (Trad.) {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, 22nd year |Main Hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] style, [[:en:Thatched roof|Thatched roof]] |Other names = Moto Ise Gekū |Festivals = [[April 26]] |Rituals = Hassaku Festival ([[September 1]]) |prefecture = {{ill|Fukuchiyama|WD=Q3355306|1=Fukuchiyama, Kyoto}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} }} '''Toyuke Daijinja''' ({{ill|豊受大神社|ja|豊受大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/豊受大神社|1=Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11633342}}) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in [[:en:Ōe|Ōe]], [[:en:Fukuchiyama|Fukuchiyama]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It holds the status of a former [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] and is one of the sites connected to the {{ill|Moto-Ise Shrines|lt=Moto-Ise|ja|元伊勢|1=Moto-Ise Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto-Ise Shrines|qq=}} tradition. == Overview == The enshrined deity, {{ill|Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}, was relocated from {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}} to the {{ill|Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|WD=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}}, according to legend. Toyuke Daijinja is considered one of the locations associated with the Moto Ise tradition, the original site of the Gekū, and is also known as the "Moto Ise Gekū." The shrine is part of the "Moto Ise Sansha" ({{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|lt=Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|lt=Moto Ise Three Shrines|ja|元伊勢三社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moto Ise Three Shrines|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) in the Ōe area of Fukuchiyama. The three shrines include: {|class="wikitable" |- ! colspan="3" | {{ill|Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja|元伊勢三社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Moto-Ise Shrines of Fukuchiyama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |- |'''{{ill|Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kōtai Shrine|lt=Kotai Shrine|ja|皇大神社 (福知山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotai Shrine|qq=}}''' |Fukuchiyama, Ōe-chō, Naikū |Origin site of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Naikū) |- |'''{{ill|Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|ja|豊受大神社|lt=Toyouke Daijinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Shrine (Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Fukuchiyama, Ōe-chō, Amatanai |Origin site of the {{ill|Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) |- |'''{{ill|Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|ja|天岩戸神社_(福知山市)|lt=Amanoiwato Shrine|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoiwato_Shrine_(Fukuchiyama)|qq=}}''' |Fukuchiyama, Ōe-chō, Busshōji |Inner Shrine’s Okumiya |} == Enshrined Deities == ; Main deity * '''{{ill|Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja|豊受姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyoukehime-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|1=Toyouke-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}''' - The deity enshrined at the {{ill|Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|ja|豊受大神宮|1=Toyōke Daijingū|2=ja|3=豊受大神宮|4=zh|5=豐受大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine of Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyōke Daijingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} (Gekū) ; Other Deities * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Hikohananinigi-no-Mikoto|ja|日子番能邇邇芸尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohananinigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニニギ}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} * {{ill|Futodama|lt=Futodama|lt=Futodama-no-Mikoto|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}} == History == According to the {{ill|Tōyuke-sha Gishiki-chō|ja|止由気宮儀式帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōyuke-sha Gishiki-chō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延暦儀式帳|1=Enryaku Gishikichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109596938}} (804 CE), Emperor Yūryaku relocated the deity Toyouke from {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|WD=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}} to {{ill|Ise Province|en|Ise Province|WD=Q864166|lt=Ise Province|1=Ise Province}} upon receiving a divine command from {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} in a dream. This became the Gekū of Ise Grand Shrine. The shrine claims to be the former site of this event. Other accounts suggest the shrine was established en route from [[Hinumanai Shrine|Hino-minai Shrine]] in {{ill|Kyōtango|en|Kyōtango|WD=Q513897|lt=Kyōtango|1=Kyōtango}} during the deity’s journey to Ise ({{ill|Kasa-gun Gazetteer|ja|加佐郡誌|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasa-gun Gazetteer|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). Another version links the shrine to {{ill|Prince Maro|ja|麻呂子親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Maro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 当麻皇子|1=Prince Maroko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487858}}, who established it after vanquishing demons in the area ({{ill|Tango Province Historical Records|ja|丹後州宮津府志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tango Province Historical Records|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the shrine received significant support from the {{ill|Miyazu Domain|en|Miyazu Domain|WD=Q3034498|lt=Miyazu Domain|1=Miyazu Domain}}, with the lord {{ill|Kyōgoku Takakuni|ja|京極高国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōgoku Takakuni|1=Kyōgoku Takakuni|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11374458}} funding the construction of the shrine's buildings in 1656 CE. In 1930, it was designated a Prefectural Shrine, and after World War II, it became affiliated with the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. == Grounds == File:Toyuke-daijinja haiden.JPG|'''Haiden and Honden''' (rear)
The honden features [[Shinmei-zukuri]] architecture with a [[:en:Thatched roof|Thatched roof]], external {{ill|Chigi|en|Chigi|WD=Q362820|lt=Chigi|1=Chigi}}, and {{ill|Katsuogi|ja|鰹木|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuogi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 千木・鰹木}} on the ridge. Traditionally, it underwent {{ill|Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Jingū Shikinen Sengū|lt=Shikinen Sengu|ja|式年遷宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinen Sengu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 遷宮}} every 60 years.Shirakawa Co., Showa 60. The current honden was reconstructed in 1874. File:Toyuke-daijinja torii.JPG|'''Torii''' - A kurokigi (blackwood) torii, similar to {{ill|Nonomiya Shrine|en|Nonomiya Shrine|WD=Q501266|lt=Nonomiya Shrine|1=Nonomiya Shrine}}. * '''{{ill|Kagura-den|en|Kagura-den|WD=Q94498|lt=Kaguraden|1=Kagura-den}}''' - Features {{ill|Hip roof|en|Hip roof|WD=Q162034|lt=Kirizuma-zukuri|1=Hip roof}} with shingled roofing. == Subsidiary Shrines == === Sessha (Branch Shrines) === Located around the main hall: * '''Taga Shrine''' - Left of Haiden * '''Tsuchi Shrine''' - Right of Haiden * '''Tsukuyomi-no-Miya''' - Rear left of Honden * '''Kaze-no-Miya''' - Rear right of Honden === Massha (End Shrines) === * Total: 37 shrines :: Arranged in a U-shape around the main hall, each massha was historically maintained by one of the shrine's 37 associated families, which also served as shrine caretakers ({{ill|Onshi|lt=Onshi|lt=Oshi|ja|御師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshi|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}). [[File:Motoise-gegu02.png|thumb|Diagram of shrine layout.]] == location == ; Address * Ōe-chō, Amatanai, Higashidaira 178-2, [[:en:Fukuchiyama|Fukuchiyama]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} ; Access {{underline|By Train}} * Nearest station: {{ill|Kyoto Tango Railway|en|Kyoto Tango Railway|WD=Q19727758|lt=Kyoto Tango Railway|1=Kyoto Tango Railway}} {{ill|Miyafuku Line|en|Miyafuku Line|WD=Q8192675|lt=Miyafuku Line|1=Miyafuku Line}} {{ill|Ōe-Kōkōmae Station|en|Ōe-Kōkōmae Station|WD=Q8081446|lt=Ōe High School-mae Station|1=Ōe-Kōkōmae Station}} (about 15 minutes on foot) {{underline|By Car}} * [[Kyoto Prefectural Route 9|Kyoto Route 9 Ayabe-Ōe-Miyazu Line]] ; Nearby * '''Miyagawa''' - A river near the shrine, sharing its name with {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa River (Mie Prefecture)|1=Miyagawa}} near Ise Grand Shrine. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == References == * {{Cite book|author=Association of Shinto Shrines Research Division|title=Jinja Meikan|publisher=Association of Shinto Shrines|date=1963}} * {{Cite book|author=Kenichi Tanigawa|title=Japan’s Gods – Shrines and Sacred Sites|volume=Vol. 7|series=San’in (Revised and Republished)|publisher=Hakusuisha|date=1985|isbn=978-4-560-02217-3}} * {{Cite book|author=Naokazu Miyaji, Ariyoshi Saeki (Supervisors)|title=Shinto Daijiten (Reprint Edition)|publisher=Rinsen Books|date=1969|isbn=978-4-653-01347-0}} == See Also == * {{ill|Hinumanai Shrine|ja|比沼麻奈為神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hinumanai Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commons|Category:original pages]] [[Category:Toyuke-daijinja}}* [https://www.city.fukuchiyama.lg.jp/soshiki/65/1115.html Moto Ise Gekū Toyuke Daijinja - Fukuchiyama City Official Homepage] * [https://www.kyoto-kankou.or.jp/info_search/?id=6222&r=1632663304.3013 Moto Ise Gekū Toyuke Daijinja - Kyoto Prefectural Tourism Guide] * [http://motoise-naiku.com/motoise3sya.html Moto Ise Naikū Kōtaijinja Official Site - Moto Ise Three Shrines] * [https://www.uminokyoto.jp/genryu/fukuchiyama/page4.html Moto Ise Three Shrines - Fukuchiyama - Sea of Kyoto (Northern Kyoto Tourism Office)] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|豊受大神社|version=85751958|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Toyuke-daijinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11633342|co|commons:Category:Toyuke-daijinja|ja|豊受大神社}} [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Miyazu Domain]] [[Category:Moto-Geku Shrines]] [[Category:Moto-Ise shrines]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Toyo'uke Daijingu Shrine]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
